mission.title: lima: UDP ports not forwarded to host
mission.description: UDP ports not forwarded to host. Requested by 23+ users.

steps.title: Check current lima setup
steps.description:
Verify your lima version and configuration:
```bash
limactl --version
```
This feature requires a working lima installation.

steps.title: Review lima configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant lima configuration:
Hi

I'm running lima v0.7.2 and can't seem to get UDP ports to forward to the host as TCP ports are.

steps.title: Apply the fix for UDP ports not forwarded to host
steps.description:
> I know you'd brought up reassembling UDP datagrams, but TCP and UDP have the same max packet size (64 KiB), so I think forwarding the packets agnostically should actually just work as expected.

Idk, I can't concentrate on this right now. You would probably have to set the MSS value in the TCP
```yaml
me@home ~ % lima nerdctl run -d \
    --name pihole \
    -p 53:53/tcp \
    -p 53:53/udp \
    -p 67:67/udp \
    -p 8080:80 \
    -e TZ="GMT" \
    -v "<base>/opt/pihole/etc-pihole/:/etc/pihole/" \
    -v "<base>/opt/pihole/etc-dnsmasq.d/:/etc/dnsmasq.d/"
    --dns=127.0.0.1 \
    --dns=1.1.1.1 \
    --restart=always \
    --hostname pi.hole \
    --cap-add=NET_ADMIN \
    -e VIRTUAL_HOST="pi.hole" \
    -e PROXY_LOCATION="pi.hole" \
    -e ServerIP="0.0.0.0" \
    pihole/pihole:latest
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "UDP ports not forwarded to host" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> I know you'd brought up reassembling UDP datagrams, but TCP and UDP have the same max packet size (64 KiB), so I think forwarding the packets agnostically should actually just work as expected.

Idk, I can't concentrate on this right now.
codeSnippets:
me@home ~ % lima nerdctl run -d \
    --name pihole \
    -p 53:53/tcp \
    -p 53:53/udp \
    -p 67:67/udp \
    -p 8080:80 \
    -e TZ="GMT" \
    -v "<base>/opt/pihole/etc-pihole/:/etc/pihole/" \
    -v "<base>/opt/pihole/etc-dnsmasq.d/:/etc/dnsmasq.d/"
    --dns=127.0.0.1 \
    --dns=1.1.1.1 \
    --restart=always \
    --hostname pi.hole \
    --cap-add=NET_ADMIN \
    -e VIRTUAL_HOST="pi.hole" \
    -e PROXY_LOCATION="pi.hole" \
    -e ServerIP="0.0.0.0" \
    pihole/pihole:latest

prerequisites.description: A working lima installation or development environment.
